task:
Build this app in Clear:

**Support Triage (AI-assisted)** (Level 9)

Build a support ticket triage system. Tickets have subject, body, from_email, category, priority, status. Use a Claude agent named 'Triage' that receives a ticket and returns a JSON object with {category, priority}. When a new ticket is POSTed, call the Triage agent to classify it before saving. Need: POST /api/tickets (auto-classifies), GET /api/tickets (list), GET /api/tickets/open.

Starting skeleton:
```clear
build for web
final_source:
build for web and javascript backend

database is local memory

owner is 'support@company.com'
allow signup and login

# Data model
create a Tickets table:
  subject, required
  body
  from_email
  category, default 'General'
  priority, default 'Normal'
  status, default 'open'

# AI Triage Agent with structured JSON output
agent 'Triage' receives ticket:
  combined = ticket's subject
  response = ask claude 'You are a support ticket triage expert. Classify this ticket. Respond with JSON: {category, priority}.' with combined returning JSON text:
    category
    priority
  send back response

# POST endpoint: receive ticket, save it with default values
when user calls POST /api/tickets sending ticket:
  try:
    classified = call 'Triage' with ticket
    set ticket's category to classified's category
    set ticket's priority to classified's priority
  catch error:
    keep ticket's category as is
    keep ticket's priority as is
  
  saved = save ticket to Tickets
  send back saved with status 201

# GET all tickets
when user calls GET /api/tickets:
  tickets = get all Tickets
  send back tickets

# GET open tickets only
when user calls GET /api/tickets/open:
  open_tickets = get all Tickets where status is 'open'
  send back open_tickets

# Frontend page
page 'Support Inbox' at '/':
  section 'New Ticket':
    'Subject' as text input saved as subject
    'Body' as text area saved as body
    'From Email' as text input saved as from_email
    
    button 'Submit Ticket':
      send {subject, body, from_email} as a new record to '/api/tickets'
      set subject to ''
      set body to ''
      set from_email to ''
  
  section 'Open Tickets':
    on page load get tickets from '/api/tickets/open'
    display tickets as table
